county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Franklin, IN. Created 1822 from unorganized territory (Delaware). Johnson County Historical Society and Museum. Franklin College (1834) archives hold local historical materials. Blue River and Youngs Creek valley settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Strong Baptist heritage — Franklin College was founded by Indiana Baptists. Camp Atterbury (WWII military installation) records. Edinburgh and Trafalgar area records. Well-preserved county records from 1823.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1822. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1822. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1822. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, order books, and judgments from 1822. Indiana Circuit Court is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, bastardy cases, and naturalization petitions (pre-1906) that identify family relationships. Order books often record road petitions naming landowners.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Johnson County Auditor, Franklin / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values from 1822. Indiana county auditor records. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Indiana State Archives holds some microfilmed county tax records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Indiana State Archives; others remain at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Johnson County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Vincennes. Knox County was established in 1790 as one of the original Northwest Territory counties — Indiana's oldest county. Vincennes was the French colonial capital and later the Indiana Territorial capital. George Rogers Clark's 1779 conquest of Fort Sackville (Vincennes). French colonial records (1732+) at Vincennes University Library and Indiana State Archives. Knox County Historical Society. Grouseland (William Henry Harrison's home). Catholic parish records in French from early 1700s. Old Cathedral Library. Extremely early records for Indiana.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1790. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage applications, licenses, and returns from 1790. Indiana County Clerk issues marriage licenses. Applications include ages, birthplaces, and parents' names (improving detail over time).
source_profiles.survival_notes: Well-preserved at County Clerk office

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1790. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Civil and criminal case files, order books, and judgments from 1790. Indiana Circuit Court is the general trial court. Early records include debt cases, property disputes, bastardy cases, and naturalization petitions (pre-1906) that identify family relationships. Order books often record road petitions naming landowners.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Court records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Knox County Auditor, Vincennes / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Tax duplicates list property owners, land descriptions, and property values from 1790. Indiana county auditor records. Early tax lists serve as census substitutes. Indiana State Archives holds some microfilmed county tax records.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Some tax duplicates at Indiana State Archives; others remain at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Knox County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.repository: Knox County Clerk, Vincennes / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court (County Clerk). Post-1906 copies at NARA Chicago. Indiana had significant German, Irish, Swiss, and later Eastern European and Mexican immigration.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Indiana naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry; some at Indiana State Archives

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana provided approximately 197,000 soldiers to the Union Army. Adjutant General records, enlistment papers, and discharge records at Indiana State Archives. Indiana Soldiers and Sailors Monument (Indianapolis). W.H.H. Terrell's 'Report of the Adjutant General of the State of Indiana' (8 vols.) published rosters.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Indiana Adjutant General records well-preserved at Indiana State Archives; indexed online

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Warsaw. Created 1835 from Elkhart and Cass counties. Named for Polish hero Tadeusz Kosciuszko. Kosciusko County Historical Society in Warsaw. Warsaw is the 'Orthopedic Capital of the World' — medical device company records. Winona Lake was a major Chautauqua site — Winona History Center records. Tippecanoe River and lakes region. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Strong Brethren (Dunkard) church records. Mentone, Pierceton, and Silver Lake area records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1835. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wills, administrations, guardianships, and estate inventories from 1835. Indiana probate is handled by the Circuit Court (County Clerk). Inventories often list personal property in detail and name heirs.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.repository: Bureau of Land Management, General Land Office Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for Kosciusko County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in LaGrange. Created 1832 from Allen and Elkhart counties. Major Amish settlement — LaGrange County has one of the largest Amish populations in Indiana. Amish church district records, family histories, and The Budget newspaper correspondence. LaGrange County Historical Society. Shipshewana Flea Market area. Pigeon River and Mongo settlement areas. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Die Botschaft newspaper community reports. Check Allen and Elkhart for pre-1832 records. Topeka and Wolcottville area records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1832. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Original federal land patents for LaGrange County. Indiana is a federal land state — most land was sold through federal land offices. Patents record first purchaser from the government. Searchable online at glorecords.blm.gov. Cross-reference with county deed records for subsequent transfers.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.source_name: Amish and Mennonite Church Records — LaGrange County
source_profiles.repository: Mennonite Historical Library, Goshen College / Heritage Historical Library, Aylmer ON / Local church records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Amish church district records, family group sheets, Budget and Die Botschaft newspaper correspondence, and Mennonite church records. Records may be in German/Pennsylvania Dutch. Mennonite Historical Library at Goshen College is a premier repository. Family histories compiled by Amish genealogists are often in private collections.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Amish records in private family collections; Mennonite Historical Library has compiled indexes and microfilm

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in LaPorte. Created 1832 from St. Joseph County. Michigan City on Lake Michigan — port and shipping records. LaPorte County Historical Society. LaPorte and Michigan City area records. Barker Mansion in Michigan City documents industrial family. Indiana State Prison (Michigan City, 1860) records. Great Sauk Trail heritage. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Strong German, Irish, and later Eastern European immigrant records. Pine Lake and Stone Lake area settlements. Check St. Joseph for pre-1832 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1832. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.survival_notes: Complete federal records — well-preserved and digitized at BLM-GLO

source_profiles.repository: NARA, Washington DC / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Potawatomi Nation records including treaties, Trail of Death removal records (1838), land allotments, and agent correspondence. 859 Potawatomi were forcibly removed from Indiana to Kansas in September 1838. Chief Menominee's band. Pokagon Band of Potawatomi retained some lands.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Federal records at NARA Record Group 75; Trail of Death records well-documented

source_profiles.repository: LaPorte County Clerk, LaPorte / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court (County Clerk). Post-1906 copies at NARA Chicago. Indiana had significant German, Irish, Swiss, and later Eastern European and Mexican immigration.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana provided approximately 197,000 soldiers to the Union Army. Adjutant General records, enlistment papers, and discharge records at Indiana State Archives. Indiana Soldiers and Sailors Monument (Indianapolis). W.H.H. Terrell's 'Report of the Adjutant General of the State of Indiana' (8 vols.) published rosters.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Indiana Adjutant General records well-preserved at Indiana State Archives; indexed online

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Crown Point. Created 1836 from Porter County. Lake County is Indiana's most urban and diverse county — Gary, East Chicago, Hammond, and Whiting. Gary was built by U.S. Steel Corporation (1906) as a company town — extensive U.S. Steel employment records. Massive immigration: Serbian, Croatian, Polish, Mexican, and African American (Great Migration) communities. Lake County Historical Society. Crown Point courthouse famous for marriages (Rudolph Valentino married here). Calumet Region Archives at Indiana University Northwest. Steel mill records essential.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1836. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival — continuous records from county formation

source_profiles.survival_notes: Probate records generally well-preserved at county level

source_profiles.source_name: Steel Industry and Immigration Records — Lake County
source_profiles.repository: Calumet Regional Archives, Indiana University Northwest / Indiana State Archives
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Gary, East Chicago, Hammond, and Whiting attracted massive immigration for steel and oil refining. U.S. Steel, Inland Steel, and Standard Oil employment records. Serbian, Croatian, Polish, Hungarian, Mexican, and African American (Great Migration) communities. Church records in multiple languages. Calumet Regional Archives holds key collections.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Factory records scattered; Calumet Regional Archives is primary repository. Some records at Indiana University Northwest.

source_profiles.repository: Lake County Clerk, Crown Point / NARA Chicago
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Declarations of intention, petitions for naturalization, and certificates. Pre-1906 records at county Circuit Court (County Clerk). Post-1906 copies at NARA Chicago. Indiana had significant German, Irish, Swiss, and later Eastern European and Mexican immigration.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many Indiana naturalization records indexed on FamilySearch and Ancestry; some at Indiana State Archives

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Bedford. Created 1818 from Orange County. Bedford is the 'Limestone Capital of the World' — limestone quarry employment records document immigrant workers including Italian stone cutters. Lawrence County Historical and Genealogical Society. Spring Mill State Park (pioneer village). East Fork White River and Salt Creek valleys. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Mitchell and Oolitic area records. Strong Methodist and Baptist church records. Check Orange County for pre-1818 records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1818. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Anderson. Created 1823 from unorganized territory (Delaware). Anderson was a major auto industry city — General Motors (Delco Remy, Guide Lamp) employment records document thousands of workers. Indiana natural gas boom county. Madison County Historical Society. Mounds State Park (Adena-Hopewell earthworks). White River valley. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Strong African American community records in Anderson. Elwood, Pendleton, and Alexandria area records. Anderson University (Church of God) archives.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1823. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

source_profiles.source_name: Automobile Industry Employment Records — Madison County
source_profiles.repository: Indiana State Library / Indiana Historical Society / Local historical societies
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Indiana was a major early automobile manufacturing center. Companies include Studebaker (South Bend), Haynes-Apperson (Kokomo), Duesenberg (Indianapolis), and later GM, Chrysler plants. Employment records, company newsletters, and union records document worker families across central Indiana.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Scattered across local repositories; Indiana State Library and Indiana Historical Society hold some collections

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Indianapolis. Created 1821 from unorganized territory (Delaware). Indianapolis is the state capital and largest city. Indiana State Archives and Indiana State Library (Genealogy Division) are both located here — primary state repositories. Indianapolis-Marion County Public Library has excellent genealogy collection. Crown Hill Cemetery records (3rd largest cemetery in US). Marion County court records from 1822. Benjamin Harrison Presidential Site. Indianapolis Motor Speedway heritage. Massive Civil War Camp Morton records. City directories from 1855. Diverse immigrant communities: German, Irish, Italian, and African American (Indiana Avenue heritage).

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1821. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Wabash and Erie Canal (longest canal ever built in North America, 468 miles) and Whitewater Canal construction records, land purchases, worker rolls, and canal-related property records. Canal workers included many Irish immigrants. Canal land grants at Indiana State Archives.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Canal Commission records at Indiana State Archives; some published by Indiana Historical Bureau

county_metadata.research_tips: County Recorder in Plymouth. Created 1835 from St. Joseph and Elkhart counties. Marshall County is historically significant for the Potawatomi Trail of Death (1838) — forced removal of 859 Potawatomi from Indiana to Kansas. Trail of Death records at NARA and Indiana State Archives. Marshall County Historical Society. Chief Menominee memorial. Yellow River and Lake Maxinkuckee (Culver) settlements. BLM-GLO federal land patents. Culver Military Academy archives. Bremen, Argos, and Bourbon area records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1835. County Recorder holds grantor/grantee indexes. Indiana deeds include warranty deeds, quit-claim deeds, and mortgage records. Federal land state — earliest deeds may reference BLM-GLO original patents.
